# Object Familiarity questionnaire {#sec-objects}

**Please use an appropriate version of the** {{< var project.number >}} **Object Experience Questionnaire** to collect information about participants' experience with objects similar to the visual stimuli used in the study.

### \>\> {{< var link.obj-exp >}} \<\<

> We have provided the template as a document and as a Google form.

<br>

::: callout-warning
# CHECK FOR MISSING DATA

While parents/guardians can choose to not respond to individual questions, please **CHECK THE FORM BEFORE THE RESPONDANT LEAVES YOUR LAB** to avoid accidental blank responses/missing data.^[In other words, please confirm that blank items are **intentional non-responses** rather than mistakes.]
:::

## Adaptations and translations

We have created an English-language version template that is suited for a United States context. If you are collecting data in a language other than English, please do your best to translate the questionnaire so that you are querying the same *construct* as the template item.

::: callout-note
# MB5 Object Experience directory

Translations/adaptations of the {{< var project.number >}} Object Experience Questionnaire will be available [**here**]({{< var url.obj-exp >}}){target="_blank"} as they become available.

> We are unable to centrally verify the accuracy of all translations/adaptations. Labs are responsible for reviewing adaptations to ensure that they are appropriate for their context and query the constructs in the original template (`mb5_object-experience-questionnaire_english`). Labs are encouraged to work with others in their geographic region to create context-appropriate versions of the questionnaire.
:::


If you have any questions about translating the questionnaire, please {{< var email.emailus >}} to discuss your concerns. Let us know if any of the questions are unclear to you---it is important that you are able to answer any questions parents/guardians may have.
